overview: Poison Storm is a Pauper combo deck that leverages Phyrexian proliferation mechanics to generate massive mana and multiply poison counters on opponents. It aims to reach lethal poison without attacking, relying on fast mana artifacts and lands to fuel its game plan. The deck uses card advantage and life gain spells to survive early disruption and execute its linear strategy.
win_conditions: Proliferate poison counters on the opponent to lethal
Multiply poison counters using proliferate spells to win without attacking

keep_criteria: a proliferate spell and a Pentad Prism
a Big Card Advantage spell like Deep Analysis or Vivisurgeon's Insight
Weather the Storm in the opener against aggressive decks
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking lands or blue sources
Hands without a clear path to mana acceleration
Hands that do not match the required speed for the matchup

examples.reason: Matches the ideal hand structure with mana acceleration, proliferation, and a poison enabler.

early_game: Play non-blue depletion lands first to enable Everflowing Chalice
Prioritize developing mana over casting poison enablers
Cast early proliferate spells if mana allows
mid_game: Deploy Pentad Prisms and Everflowing Chalices to generate exponential mana
Use proliferate spells like Experimental Augury to control sequences and draw cards
Multiply poison counters and artifact/land counters simultaneously
late_game: Chain proliferate spells to reach lethal poison quickly
Cast Weather the Storm at the end of high-spell turns to gain massive life
Resolve key spells through disruption using sideboarded counterspells

key_cards.name: Pentad Prism
key_cards.notes: Best mana producer in the deck; proliferating it makes 2+ mana value spells effectively free.

key_cards.name: Everflowing Chalice
key_cards.notes: Provides massive colorless mana after 1-2 turns; helps pay colorless requirements.

key_cards.name: Prologue to Phyresis
key_cards.notes: Quickest way to give opponent a poison counter; blue-friendly and mana efficient.

key_cards.name: Vivisurgeon's Insight
key_cards.notes: Strong early proliferate spell; generates massive card advantage and mana back.

key_cards.name: Weather the Storm
key_cards.notes: Necessary evil to gain 24+ life and survive pressure when casting many spells.

key_cards.name: Serrated Arrows
key_cards.notes: Deals with threats; arrowhead counters and -1/-1 counters can be proliferated.

key_cards.name: Deep Analysis
key_cards.notes: Alternative to Vivisurgeon's Insight; better against counterspells as it's two separate spells.

key_cards.name: Bring the Ending
key_cards.notes: Easier to cast counterspell for winning counter wars; corrupted effect triggers naturally.

sequencing_tips: Play non-blue depletion lands first to enable Everflowing Chalice and maximize Pentad Prism
Prioritize developing mana over casting poison enablers early
Use proliferate spells to multiply both poison counters and artifact/land counters for mana generation
Cast Weather the Storm at the end of turns with high spell counts to maximize life gain
Split card draw into multiple spells like Deep Analysis to bypass counterspells

matchups.advice: Deal with initial wave, keep depletion-heavy lands to explode after they cast Raze, side in Hydroblast.
Remove bad artifacts like Everflowing Chalice and Serrated Arrows to avoid dead cards, side in Anoint with Affliction to handle early Faeries.
Fairly free matchup, side in Moment's Peace as extra Weather the Storm and Bring the Ending to handle Pyroblast.
Side in Deep Analysis over Vivisurgeon's Insight to split card draw, use Bring the Ending for crucial spells.
No sideboard changes needed, optionally swap Serrated Arrows for Anoint with Affliction.

overview_evidence.evidence_span: You can proliferate your opponent to lethal Poison Counters without ever needing to attack them.

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: You can proliferate your opponent to lethal Poison Counters without ever needing to attack them.

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: playing the non-blue depletion land first is generally correct, as is allows you to cast Everflowing Chalice and maximizes Pentad Prism

Developing your mana is much more important. This is because you have access to so many proliferate spells, that if you need to deploy 3-4 of these to develop your mana, there is still plenty more in the deck

common_threats_evidence.evidence_span: Looking at the card choices lets us see how we can survive in a Mono Red and Mono Blue Mine Field.
Walls Combo along with other combo decks are particularly problematic for this deck
